subtitle: Franchise Replication Guide
confidentialityNotice: Confidential & Proprietary - For authorized personnel only.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.content:
Mission

ICE (International Cultural Exchange) is dedicated to empowering young adults and professionals to achieve their dreams of international cultural exchange, education, and career advancement through world-class services and cutting-edge technology.

Vision

To be the leading agency in Latin America for cultural exchange programs, recognized for our operational excellence, AI-driven automation, and unwavering commitment to client success.

Target Market

SegmentDescription
University StudentsAged 18-28, seeking Work & Travel USA or summer internship programs.
ProfessionalsYoung professionals exploring Au Pair or career development programs abroad.
FamiliesParents securing cultural exchange opportunities for their children.
Agencies & PartnersTravel agencies and educational institutions seeking a white-label visa solution.

Value Proposition

ICE offers a unique, technology-forward approach to cultural exchange services:

  1. AI-Powered Visa Rescheduling: Our proprietary automation system monitors visa appointment portals 24/7 and automatically reschedules appointments to earlier dates.
  2. End-to-End Client Support: From lead capture to program completion, clients are guided by dedicated agents.
  3. Unified Portal System: A single platform for students, agents, and administrators to manage documents, track progress, and communicate.
  4. Data-Driven Operations: Lead scoring, agent performance tracking, and analytics for continuous improvement.

Franchise Opportunity Overview

This manual serves as the foundational guide for replicating the ICE operational model. A franchise package includes:

  • Full Technology Stack: Access to the Visa Rescheduler Bot, CRM, and Student Portal.
  • Operational Playbook: This manual, covering all workflows and procedures.
  • Training Program: Comprehensive onboarding for all roles.
  • Brand & Marketing Assets: Collateral, templates, and campaign guides.
  • Ongoing Support: Technical support, software updates, and strategic guidance.

For commercial terms (Sale, Rental, Subscription), please contact: support@apexaisolutions.dev

sections.title: Organizational Culture
sections.content:
Core Values

ValueDescription
Client-CentricityEvery decision is made with the client's success in mind.
IntegrityWe operate with transparency and honesty in all interactions.
InnovationWe leverage technology to improve efficiency and outcomes.
CollaborationWe work as one team, across departments and roles.
AccountabilityWe take ownership of our responsibilities and results.

Code of Conduct

All ICE team members are expected to adhere to the following standards:

  1. Professionalism: Maintain a professional demeanor in all client and team interactions.
  2. Confidentiality: Protect client data and company secrets.
  3. Respect: Treat colleagues, clients, and partners with respect and courtesy.
  4. Punctuality: Honor all scheduled meetings and deadlines.
  5. Continuous Improvement: Actively seek to improve skills and processes.

Communication Standards

ChannelPurposeResponse Time SLA
EmailFormal communication, documentation< 24 hours
WhatsAppQuick internal coordination, urgent client matters< 1 hour
Portal ChatClient support inquiries< 4 hours
Daily HuddleTeam sync, priorities, blockersN/A (Scheduled)
Weekly MeetingPerformance review, planningN/A (Scheduled)

Quality Standards

  • Lead Response Time: All new leads must be contacted within 4 hours of entry into the system.
  • Client Satisfaction Score: Target a minimum 4.5/5 rating on post-service surveys.
  • First Call Resolution: Aim for 80% of client inquiries resolved on the first contact.

sections.title: Roles and Responsibilities
sections.content:
This section defines the job descriptions for each role within the ICE organization, structured in a professional tier-based hierarchy for scalable operations.

Director / CTOSUPER_ADMINFull system owner and strategic leader.Strategic direction, P&L ownership, investor relations, technology oversight, cross-departmental alignment.
Operations ManagerADMINDay-to-day operational lead.Manages all departments, handles escalations, approves policies, defines KPIs, conducts performance reviews.

Tier 2: Supervision & Coordination

RoleSystem RoleDescriptionKey Responsibilities
SupervisorSUPERVISORWorkforce coordinator.Monitors real-time agent performance, assigns tasks, conducts QA, runs daily briefings, ensures alignment with business goals.
Team LeadTEAM_LEADSenior Agent (optional tier).Handles complex or escalated client cases, mentors junior agents, provides first-level support.

Tier 3: Operational Staff

SpecializationDescription

sections.title: Workforce Management
sections.content:
This section details the processes and tools for managing the ICE workforce, inspired by professional call center and sales operations.

Daily Operations Rhythm

Morning Briefing ("Huddle")

  • Frequency: Daily, at the start of each shift.
  • Duration: 10-15 minutes.
  • Led By: Supervisor.

Agenda: Review previous day's performance, announce today's targets, highlight urgent leads/escalations, address agent questions, motivational close.

End-of-Day Wrap-Up

Agents update lead statuses, log notes, and flag any end-of-day hand-offs. Supervisor reviews dashboards and prepares notes for next morning briefing.

Task Assignment Methodology

Supervisors use the Agent Control Panel (/portal/agents) to manage workload.

  1. Current Workload: Agents with lower active task counts receive new assignments first.
  2. Specialization Match: Leads are matched to agents specializing in the relevant program.
  3. Round Robin: For unspecialized leads, distribute evenly across available agents.
  4. Manual Override: Supervisors can manually re-assign leads based on judgment.

Quality Assurance (QA) Process

  1. Sampling: Supervisor reviews a random sample of 5-10% of agent interactions per month.
  2. Scoring: Each interaction is scored on Professionalism, Accuracy, Client Rapport, Closure (1-5 each).
  3. Feedback: Results shared individually. Trends discussed in weekly team meetings.
  4. Action Items: Agents scoring below threshold receive targeted coaching.

Performance Monitoring (Real-Time Dashboards)

MetricDescription
Agent StatusOnline, Offline, On Break. (Agent.isOnline, Agent.lastSeen)
Lead Response TimeTime from lead creation to first contact.
Conversion Rate% of leads converted per agent. (Lead.stage = 'CONVERTED')
Task CompletionOpen vs. completed tasks. (Task.status)
Daily ActivityCalls made, emails sent, leads contacted.

Escalation Matrix

A clear escalation path ensures client issues are resolved efficiently.

LevelHandlerTrigger
L1AgentInitial client contact, standard inquiries.
L2Team LeadComplex queries, agent needs guidance, first escalation.
L3SupervisorUnresolved L2 issues, complaints, policy exceptions.
L4Operations ManagerCritical issues, VIP clients, systemic problems.
L5Director/CTOLegal issues, PR crises, strategic decisions.

callout.title: Escalation Matrix
callout.content: A clear escalation path ensures client issues are resolved efficiently: L1 (Agent) → L2 (Team Lead) → L3 (Supervisor) → L4 (Operations Manager) → L5 (Director/CTO).

sections.title: Service Catalog
sections.content:
Core Service: Automated Visa Rescheduling

The ICE Visa Rescheduler Bot is a proprietary automation system that monitors US Embassy visa appointment portals worldwide. It automatically identifies earlier available appointment slots and reschedules the client to the better date.

How It Works

Client -> ICE System: Register
ICE System -> Queue: Create Job
Check Loop: System -> Portal -> Available?
If Yes: Reschedule -> Notify Client

Supported Consulates

ConsulateCode
US Embassy MadridUS_EMBASSY_MADRID
US Embassy BarcelonaUS_EMBASSY_BARCELONA
US Embassy Mexico CityUS_EMBASSY_MEXICO_CITY
US Embassy LondonUS_EMBASSY_LONDON
US Embassy MumbaiUS_EMBASSY_MUMBAI
US Embassy DelhiUS_EMBASSY_DELHI
US Embassy FrankfurtUS_EMBASSY_FRANKFURT

Key Features

  • Human-like Behavior: Randomized delays, realistic browser fingerprints, anti-detection measures.
  • Smart Scheduling: Preference-based slot selection (time windows, blackout dates).
  • Screenshot Audit Trail: Visual proof of all automation steps stored for compliance.
  • Multi-Channel Notifications: Email (via Resend/SMTP) and SMS (via Twilio).

Cultural Exchange Programs

Work & Travel USA

  • Target Audience: University students aged 18-28.
  • Duration: Summer season (typically 3-4 months).
  • Service Includes: Job placement assistance, J-1 visa sponsorship, pre-departure orientation, in-country support.

Au Pair Programs

  • Target Audience: Young adults (18-26) seeking childcare exchange.
  • Duration: 1-2 years.
  • Service Includes: Host family matching, visa sponsorship, cultural orientation.

Visa Advisory

  • Target Audience: Any individual requiring visa consultation.
  • Service Includes: Document preparation, interview coaching, consulate appointment management.

Student Services

  • Document Management: Secure upload, verification, and storage of required documents.
  • Progress Tracking: Students can view their application status, GPA, and credits via the Student Portal.
  • Enrollment Management: Link students to specific programs with start/end dates and completion tracking.

Service Pricing (Placeholder)

Pricing is determined by commercial agreements and may vary by franchise location. Contact support@apexaisolutions.dev for rate cards.

sections.title: Lead Acquisition and Management
sections.content:
This section outlines the process for capturing, scoring, assigning, and managing leads from initial contact to qualification.

Lead Sources

Leads enter the ICE system from multiple channels:

SourceDescriptionSystem Field
EventsUniversity fairs, expos, presentations.event_fair_name
WebsiteContact forms, landing pages.website_form
Social MediaFacebook, Instagram campaigns.social_facebook, social_instagram
ReferralsExisting client referrals.referral
PartnersAgency or institutional partners.partner_agency_name

Lead Capture (ETL Process)

  1. Data Ingestion: Leads from external sources (spreadsheets, API webhooks) are ingested via ETL jobs.
  2. Data Normalization: Raw data is mapped to the Person and Lead models.
  3. Deduplication: System checks for existing Person by email or cedula.
  4. Creation: New Lead record created and linked to Person, Service, and Event (if applicable).

Lead Scoring

Leads are automatically scored (0-100) to prioritize follow-up.

Scoring Components & Weights

ComponentWeightDescription
Fit25%Program compatibility, age, country, eligibility.
Intent25%Program interest indicated, budget signals.
Engagement20%Email opens, replies, calls answered, events attended.
Source Quality10%Historical conversion rate of the source.
Recency10%Time since last contact (recent = higher).
Agent Performance10%Assigned agent's historical conversion rate.

Conversion Probability & Estimated Value

  • conversionProbability: Derived from score via logistic transform.
  • estimatedValue: conversionProbability * expectedDealValue

Lead Assignment

Leads are assigned to Agents based on:

  1. Auto-Assignment (Round Robin): New leads distributed evenly.
  2. Skill-Based Routing: Leads for specific programs routed to specialized agents.
  3. Manual Assignment: Supervisor assigns lead via Agent Control Panel.

System Field: Lead.assignedAgentId

Kanban Pipeline Stages

Leads progress through defined stages:

StageDescriptionTriggers
NEWFreshly captured lead, not yet contacted.Lead creation.
CONTACTEDAgent has made first contact.Agent logs call/email.
QUALIFIEDLead confirmed as a genuine opportunity.Agent qualifies (interest + ability).
OPPORTUNITYActive negotiation, proposal sent.Proposal or contract sent.
CONVERTEDSuccessfully enrolled/signed.Payment received or contract signed.
LOSTLead declined or disqualified.Agent marks as lost (with reason).

System Field: Lead.stage

Lead List & Filtering (Agent View)

Agents access their assigned leads via /ice-leads or /portal/leads:

  • Filters: Stage, Source, Score Range, Date Range, Assigned Agent.
  • Sorting: By Name, Last Contact Date, Lead Score.
  • Quick Actions: Log Call, Send Email, Change Stage, Reassign.

sections.title: Client Follow-Up and Nurturing
sections.content:
This section describes the standard procedures for contacting and nurturing leads after acquisition.

Follow-Up Cadence

A structured follow-up cadence ensures leads are contacted consistently. If no response after Day 14, lead may be moved to a "Dormant" status for future re-engagement campaigns.

DayActionChannel
Day 0Initial contact attemptCall
Day 0If no answer, send intro messageWhatsApp / Email
Day 1Second contact attemptCall
Day 2Follow-up value messageEmail
Day 4Third contact attemptCall
Day 7Nurture content (success stories, program info)Email
Day 14Final warm outreachCall / Email

Communication Channels

ChannelUse CaseBest Practices
Phone CallPrimary contact for new leads, urgent matters.Be prepared with lead info, personalize greeting.
WhatsAppQuick follow-ups, reminders, document requests.Keep messages concise, respond within 1 hour.
EmailFormal proposals, document sharing, nurture content.Use templates, include clear CTA.
Portal NotificationsSystem-generated updates (status changes, appointments).Automatic.

Tracking Engagement

Agent activity and lead engagement are tracked in the system:

FieldDescription
Lead.lastContactedAtTimestamp of last agent interaction.
Lead.contactAttemptsNumber of contact attempts made.
Lead.followupStatusJSON object with detailed status notes.
AuditLogRecord of all actions taken on the lead.

Escalation Procedures

  1. Stalled Lead: Agent flags lead for Supervisor review. Supervisor may reassign or attempt contact.
  2. Upset or Complaining Client: Escalate immediately to Team Lead (L2) or Supervisor (L3). Document interaction thoroughly.
  3. Information Dispute: Escalate to Operations Manager with supporting documentation.

Nurture Campaigns (Dormant Leads)

Leads that go cold are not discarded. Marketing runs periodic re-engagement campaigns: Email Newsletters, Special Promotions, Event Invitations.

sections.title: Client Onboarding and Enrollment
sections.content:
This section covers the conversion of a qualified lead into an enrolled student/client.

Conversion Workflow

Lead -> Accepts? -> (Yes) Create Student -> Collect Docs -> Invoice -> Payment -> Enroll -> Active
(No) -> Lost

Step 1: Client Accepts Offer

  • Agent presents program details, pricing, and terms.
  • Client verbally or in writing agrees to proceed.
  • Lead stage updated to OPPORTUNITY then CONVERTED.

Step 2: Create Student Record

When a lead converts, a Student record is created:

  1. Agent initiates creation via portal.
  2. System links Student to existing Person.
  3. Assigns studentNumber.
  4. Sets initial status = 'ACTIVE'.

Key Fields: Student.id, Student.personId, Student.studentNumber, Student.program, Student.status.

Step 3: Document Collection

Clients upload required documents via the Student Portal.

Common Document Types

Document TypeCodeRequired For
Passport CopyPASSPORTAll programs
University TranscriptTRANSCRIPTWork & Travel
Financial StatementFINANCIAL_STATEMENTVisa applications
DS-160 ConfirmationDS160US Visa
Photo (Visa Format)PHOTOAll visas
Signed ContractCONTRACTAll programs

Document Workflow

  1. Upload: Client uploads via portal (Status: PENDING).
  2. Agent Review: Agent reviews for completeness.
  3. Approve/Reject: Status updated (with comments).

Step 4: Generate Invoice

An invoice is created linked to the client and program. Status: PENDING until payment.

Step 5: Payment & Enrollment

  1. Client makes payment.
  2. Accountant confirms payment.
  3. Invoice PAID.
  4. Enrollment record created.

Step 6: Welcome & Onboarding

  • Client receives welcome email with portal access.
  • Assigned agent introduces next steps.
  • Client becomes an Active Student.

sections.title: Visa Rescheduling Operations
sections.content:
This section details the operational workflow for the ICE Visa Rescheduler Bot, the core proprietary product.

Overview

  1. Logs into US visa appointment portals using client credentials.
  2. Monitors for earlier available appointment slots.
  3. Automatically reschedules the appointment if a better slot is found.
  4. Notifies the client via Email/SMS.

Operational Workflow

Agent -> System: Register
System -> Queue: Create Job
Worker loop: Portal Login -> Check Dates -> Available?
If Yes: Reschedule -> Notify

Step 1: Applicant Registration

  1. Agent creates Person, Appointment, Preference, and Credential records.

Step 2: Job Creation

System creates a Job in the BullMQ queue (type: 'RESCHEDULE'). Job is scheduled to run at intervals defined by SearchRule.

Step 3: Automated Monitoring

The Worker service (Playwright) picks up job, launches headless browser, logs in, searches for appointments. Takes screenshot at each step for audit trail.

Step 4: Rescheduling

If an earlier slot is found: Worker completes the reschedule flow, receives new confirmation, creates RescheduleAttempt record, updates Appointment, triggers notification.

Step 5: Notifications

Clients are notified via multi-channel system: Email (Resend API or SMTP), SMS (Twilio).

Monitoring & Audit

  • Dashboard: /dashboard shows active applicants, queue depth, success rates.
  • Screenshots: Stored in screenshots/ directory.
  • Audit Log: All actions logged to AuditLog for compliance.

Error Handling & Escalation

Error TypeAction
Login FailureNotify agent to verify credentials. Mark job as failed.
Portal UnavailableRetry with exponential backoff. Alert Supervisor after N failures.
Captcha DetectedLog and pause. May require manual intervention.
Account LockedImmediately notify client and agent. Pause all jobs for that applicant.

callout.title: Core Product Workflow
callout.content: Agent Registers Applicant → System Creates Job → Worker Monitors Portal (Loop) → If Earlier Date Found: Reschedule & Notify → Log Attempt Result

sections.title: Student Lifecycle Management
sections.content:
Student Statuses

StatusDescription
ACTIVECurrently enrolled and in-program.
INACTIVEPaused or temporarily deferred.
GRADUATEDSuccessfully completed the program.
WITHDRAWNLeft the program before completion.

Student Portal Overview

Enrolled students access the Student Portal (/student-portal) to: View their profile and program details, Upload and manage documents, Track application status and progress, View upcoming appointments or deadlines, Contact their assigned agent.

Document Management

Uploading Documents

  1. Student logs into portal.
  2. Navigates to "My Documents".
  3. Selects document type and uploads file.
  4. Document saved to MinIO storage with metadata in StudentDocument.

Document Approval Workflow

Pending → Agent Review → Approved or Rejected.

Document Types

TypeRequired For
PASSPORTAll
TRANSCRIPTWork & Travel
DS160US Visa
FINANCIAL_STATEMENTVisa
CONTRACTAll
OFFER_LETTERUniversity Programs

Progress Tracking

For academic programs, students can track: currentSemester, gpa, creditsCompleted, creditsTotal, Enrollment.completionRate.

Program Completion

  1. Agent or system updates Student.status = 'GRADUATED'.
  2. Enrollment.status = 'COMPLETED'.
  3. Completion certificate or documentation generated (if applicable).
  4. Student added to Alumni pool for referrals and testimonials.

Handling Withdrawals

  1. Agent updates Student.status = 'WITHDRAWN'.
  2. Logs reason in notes.
  3. Refund policy triggered (if applicable).
  4. Lead record may be flagged for future re-engagement.

sections.title: Technology Stack
sections.content:

StylingTailwindCSS3.xUtility-first CSS
BackendNext.js API Routes15.xREST API endpoints
ORMPrisma6.xDatabase management

QueueBullMQ5.xJob queue for automation tasks
Cache/SessionRedis6+Caching and queue backend
Browser AutomationPlaywright1.xVisa portal automation
EmailResend / NodemailerLatestEmail notifications
SecretsHashiCorp VaultLatestCredential encryption

System Architecture Diagram

ICE Portal Frontend (Next.js)
|
Authentication (NextAuth.js)
|
API Gateway
|
Business Logic (Student Mgmt, Visa System, Agent Panel)
|
Service Orchestration (BullMQ)
|
Data Access (Prisma)
|
PostgreSQL + Redis

System RoleAccess Level

ADMINOperations management. All modules except system config.
SUPERVISORAgent management, lead oversight, QA.

MARKETINGCampaigns, analytics, lead sources.

sections.title: Agent Control Panel
sections.content:
This section describes the Agent Control Panel used by Supervisors and Admins to manage the workforce.

Overview

The Agent Control Panel is accessible at /portal/agents and provides: Real-time agent status monitoring, Performance metrics and analytics, Task management and assignment, Schedule and calendar views.

Agent List View

ColumnDescription
NameAgent full name.
Departmente.g., Commercial & Processes.
RoleAgent, Team Lead, Supervisor.
StatusOnline, Offline, On Break, Training.
WorkloadNumber of active leads/tasks.
CapacityMax capacity setting.
Last ActivityTimestamp of last system action.

Performance Dashboard

MetricDescription
Leads AssignedTotal leads assigned in period.
Leads ConvertedTotal successful conversions.
Conversion Rate(Converted / Assigned) × 100.
Avg Response TimeTime to first contact.
Customer SatisfactionAverage rating from surveys.
Tasks CompletedNumber of tasks marked done.

Task Management

FieldDescription
TitleTask name.
PriorityLow, Medium, High, Urgent.
StatusPending, In Progress, Completed.
Due DateDeadline.

Real-Time Monitoring

Supervisors can see live agent activity: Online/Offline Status, Current Task, Session Tracking.

Reporting & Export

  • Export to CSV/Excel: Download agent performance reports.
  • Scheduled Reports: Configure automated email reports (planned feature).

sections.title: Franchise Model
sections.content:
This section outlines the franchise replication model for ICE.

Franchise Package Overview

ComponentDescription
Technology PlatformFull access to ICE Portal, Visa Rescheduler Bot, CRM, Student Portal.
Operational ManualThis comprehensive guide.
Training ProgramOnline and on-site training for all roles.
Brand AssetsLogo, marketing templates, collateral.
Ongoing SupportTechnical support, software updates, strategic advice.

Setup Requirements

Technical Infrastructure

RequirementSpecification
ServerLinux VPS (min 4 cores, 16GB RAM).
DatabasePostgreSQL 14+.
RedisVersion 6+.
DomainCustom domain with SSL.
EmailBusiness email with API.

Legal & Business

  • Business registration in target country.
  • Compliance with local data protection laws (GDPR, etc.).
  • Signed franchise agreement with Apex AI Solutions LLC.

Commercial Models

Specific pricing is subject to negotiation. Contact support@apexaisolutions.dev for details.

Option A: Outright Sale

  • One-time payment for perpetual license.
  • Includes initial setup and training.
  • Annual maintenance fee.

Option B: Subscription / Rental

  • Monthly or annual fee.
  • Includes all software, updates, and support.

Option C: Revenue Share

  • Lower fixed fees.
  • Percentage of franchise revenue shared.

Franchise Owner Responsibilities

  1. Hire and manage local staff.
  2. Adhere to ICE operational standards.
  3. Maintain platform infrastructure.
  4. Report performance metrics monthly.
  5. Pay franchise fees.

sections.title: Training Requirements
sections.content:
This section outlines the training program for onboarding new franchise staff.

Training Curriculum Overview

ModuleTarget RoleDurationFormat
ICE Company OverviewAll2 hoursOnline
System NavigationAll4 hoursOnline + Hands-on
Lead ManagementAgents, Ops4 hoursOnline + Hands-on
Visa OperationsAgents, Ops6 hoursOnline + Hands-on
Agent Control PanelSupervisors3 hoursHands-on
System AdminAdmins6 hoursHands-on

Training by Role

Agent Training Path

  1. ICE Company Overview
  2. System Navigation
  3. Lead Management
  4. Client Communication
  5. Visa Operations
  6. Document Management
  7. Compliance & Security

Total: ~24 hours

Supervisor Training Path

  1. Complete Agent Training Path
  2. Workforce Management
  3. Agent Control Panel
  4. Advanced Reporting

Total: ~31 hours

Admin Training Path

  1. Complete Supervisor Training Path
  2. System Administration
  3. User Management
  4. Incident Response

Total: ~39 hours

Certification

  • ICE Certified Agent/Supervisor/Admin badge.
  • Certificate of completion.
  • Role permissions enabled.

Ongoing Training

  • Quarterly Refreshers: Updates on system features.
  • New Feature Training: Major updates.
  • Performance Coaching: Based on QA.

How to Request New Modules
